target.title: refactor: add SQLite session store foundation
target.body:
## What

Path 3 / PR 3.2 for #88838 adds the unused, additive SQLite session/transcript store foundation behind the 3.1a/3.1b accessor seam. It introduces the per-agent SQLite schema, generated Kysely types, SQLite-backed session/transcript helper module, SQLite checkpoint branch/restore support for validation branches, and shared conformance coverage while preserving the current file-backed runtime behavior.

## Why

The SQLite storage flip should not land in the same PR as caller rewrites or legacy migration work. This foundation lets disposable validation branches exercise the future SQLite backend against the existing accessor seam while production runtime remains file-backed until later Path 3 slices complete caller adoption, doctor migration, and the atomic storage flip.

## Changes

- Added per-agent session/transcript SQLite schema
- Added file/SQLite accessor conformance tests
- Added transcript read-scope SQLite support
- Added canonical `sessions.json` path parity
- Added `preserveActivity` parity for SQLite patches
- Serialized SQLite entry and transcript writes
- Added exact-key SQLite session lookup
- Added SQLite transcript row mutation helpers
- Added SQLite checkpoint branch/restore helpers
- Excluded runtime storage wiring and migration

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.sql` | Adds session/transcript tables |
| `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts` | Adds per-agent DB lifecycle and schema migration/backfill |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts` | Implements additive SQLite helper surface for the current foundation slice |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts` | Shares file/SQLite behavior and checkpoint branch/restore coverage |

## Schema Audit

The refreshed schema has durable homes for the data currently needed by this foundation slice: `sessions` stores normalized session identity/status/routing fields, `session_routes` maps persisted keys to session ids, `session_entries.entry_json` preserves the full `SessionEntry` object, `transcript_events` stores ordered transcript rows, `transcript_event_identities` stores event ids/parent links/message idempotency keys, and `conversations` plus `session_conversations` cover future conversation linkage.

The current 3.1b accessor seam is broader than this helper module. Newer storage-sized operations such as transcript-turn persistence, manual trim, reset/delete lifecycle, restart recovery, reply-session initialization, and plugin-host cleanup can be represented with the existing tables, mostly through `session_entries.entry_json` plus transcript rows, but they still need SQLite adapter methods before the production storage flip. That is adapter coverage work, not a schema gap found in this refresh.

## Out Of Scope

Production storage flip/runtime wiring, doctor migration/import, runtime dual-read/fallback, and complete SQLite implementations for every post-3.1b accessor operation remain out of scope for this additive foundation PR.

## Evidence

- Rebased onto `upstream/main` `3ab8d6aa609ab7f1a6bee14500694bd7ca243489`
- `node scripts/generate-kysely-types.mjs --verify`
- `node scripts/check-kysely-guardrails.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.sql src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.generated.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.generated.d.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --engine codex --no-web-search --thinking codex=low` returned clean

target.title: fix(memory): keep shared QMD manager alive on active-memory recall timeout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96455. After an `active-memory` embedded recall run times out, chat-session `memory_search` returns `"memory search manager is closed"` even though `openclaw memory search --agent main` keeps working against the same QMD backend. The first closed-manager result can also trip the 60s `memory_search` cooldown, so recovery looks sticky until the gateway restarts.

Root cause: active-memory's recall-timeout cleanup (`scheduleMemorySearchCleanupAfterTimeout`) called `closeActiveMemorySearchManager`, which fully retired the **agent-scoped shared QMD manager** that chat `memory_search` and the startup timers/watchers depend on. A request-scoped recall timeout was tearing down long-lived shared state.

## Why This Change Was Made

The cleanup's legitimate job (introduced in #84048) is to release the **disposable local builtin index/embedding providers** a hung recall can leave open — not to retire the shared manager. The fix keeps the shipped close contract intact and does not grow the SDK surface:

- `closeActiveMemorySearchManager` gains a backward-compatible optional `scope?: "manager" | "index-managers"`. Default `"manager"` is the unchanged full-close behavior. `"index-managers"` releases **only** the disposable local index managers (a narrow `releaseMemoryIndexManagers` path in memory-core) and leaves the shared QMD manager alive.
- active-memory's recall-timeout cleanup now calls `closeActiveMemorySearchManager({ ..., scope: "index-managers" })`.
- No new public SDK export is added (the public callable export count is unchanged), so the prior review's `merge-risk: compatibility` (expanded plugin-facing API surface) no longer applies.

The recall's own in-flight QMD query is already cancelled via the embedded run abort signal, so the preserved manager stays healthy — it is the same long-lived manager CLI search and startup timers use successfully.

Addresses the prior review:
- **Compatibility / API surface:** reworked from a new SDK facade to a backward-compatible optional param — no export-count growth.
- **P1 (active-memory timeout test):** the timeout-cleanup test now asserts the `scope: "index-managers"` call, proving the new path.
- **P2 (absent release hook):** intentional no-op with a documenting comment — falling back to full-close would reintroduce #96455.

## User Impact

Users with `active-memory` enabled and a QMD-backed `memory_search` no longer lose chat memory recall after an active-memory recall timeout. The disposable local index/embedding cleanup from #84048 is preserved; only the over-broad shared-manager teardown is removed. No config or API removal; one additive optional param on an existing facade.

## Evidence

Reworked on latest `upstream/main`. Single commit; +134 / -11 across 13 files; SDK callable-export surface unchanged.

### Real QMD-backed runtime proof (qmd 2.5.3)

Installed real `@tobilu/qmd` 2.5.3 (BM25 `searchMode: "search"`). Drove the **real, un-mocked** memory-core `FallbackMemoryManager` (QMD-backed) through the recall-timeout cleanup path vs the full close:

```
[proof] manager#1: FallbackMemoryManager error: (none)
[proof] BEFORE cleanup: search outcome: {"ok":true,"hits":0}
[proof] AFTER releaseMemoryIndexManagers: sameManager: true
[proof] AFTER cleanup: search outcome: {"ok":true,"hits":0}
[proof] AFTER full close: old-manager search outcome: {"ok":false,"message":"memory search manager is closed"}
[proof] after close, fresh get is new manager: true
```

- After the recall-timeout release path: the **same** shared manager is preserved and `search` stays usable — no `"memory search manager is closed"`.
- After a full close (the old behavior): the manager throws exactly `"memory search manager is closed"` — the issue's literal symptom — and a fresh manager is created.

Real qmd BM25 retrieval is healthy on the manager's own index (the `hits:0` above is a separate qmd multi-collection/BM25-scoring nuance on the manager's filtered CLI path, unrelated to manager liveness):

```
$ qmd search "Falcon launch"   # against the manager's own index.sqlite
qmd://memory-root-main/MEMORY.md:3 #e5bc2f
Title: Memory

Project Falcon launch window opens in March. The methalox engine passed static fire.
```

### Regression tests

```
extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts ... 37 passed
src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts ........................ 12 passed (incl. scope-routing)
src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.test.ts ................. 4 passed  (incl. scope passthrough)
extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts ................... 152 passed (timeout asserts scope)
test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts ............. 7 passed  (callable-export budget unchanged)
```

- `search-manager.test.ts`: full-close still closes the QMD manager + new "releaseMemoryIndexManagers ... keeps the shared qmd manager alive (#96455)".
- `memory-runtime.test.ts`: routes `scope: "index-managers"` to the release path without retiring the shared manager.

### Local validation

```
tsgo:core                          OK
oxlint (changed files)             clean
oxfmt --check (changed files)      clean
plugin-sdk:api:gen + :check        OK (no new callable export)
```

target.title: feat(discord): show subagent progress
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Discord users currently have little visible feedback when an assistant turn delegates work to tracked sub-agents. Long-running turns can look idle after the initial message even though background sub-agents are still running, and a sub-agent failure is not visible on the original Discord message.

Discord also cannot represent concurrent work by adding the same emoji reaction multiple times from the same bot/user, so progress needs a count-based reaction model instead of repeated identical reactions.

## What This Adds

This PR adds Discord-scoped, best-effort sub-agent progress feedback on the original inbound Discord message. The feature is opt-in with `channels.discord.subagentProgress.enabled=true`.

When enabled:

- one configurable count reaction shows the number of currently active tracked sub-agents (`1️⃣` … `🔟` by default)
- count changes replace the previous count reaction instead of accumulating multiple ordinal reactions
- zero active tracked sub-agents removes the count reaction
- any non-OK completion leaves a durable configurable failure marker (`🔴` by default)
- Discord typing is kept alive independently while tracked sub-agents remain active, then stops when all tracked work finishes or the safety TTL expires

The implementation threads the source Discord message id through native `sessions_spawn`, tracked ACP spawn, requester origin metadata, and internal Discord progress hook handling so the Discord extension can target the correct parent message. Non-Discord callers and callers without a source message id keep existing behavior.

## User Impact

Discord users get immediate, lightweight progress feedback during delegated work without waiting for the final assistant reply:

- `1️⃣`, `2️⃣`, etc. show the current active tracked sub-agent count for the turn
- the count reaction is replaced as the active count changes
- the count reaction disappears when no tracked sub-agents remain active
- `🔴` remains if any tracked sub-agent ends with a non-OK outcome
- typing stays active while tracked sub-agents are still running

Operators can configure the behavior with `channels.discord.subagentProgress`, including reaction progress, count emoji, failure emoji, typing keepalive, interval, and max-duration TTL.

The feature is best-effort and Discord-only. Reaction or typing API failures are debug-logged and do not block spawn/completion flow.

## Implementation Notes

- Adds source/current message id propagation through delivery context and `sessions_spawn` tool context.
- Carries that id into native sub-agent spawn and tracked ACP registration.
- Keeps public plugin hook/requester metadata narrow while the Discord extension uses an internal Discord-only event shape for source-message progress targeting.
- Emits progress cleanup at run completion even when ordinary `subagent_ended` delivery is deferred.
- Adds a Discord progress tracker keyed by account id, parent channel id, typing/thread target id, and source message id to avoid thread collisions.
- Tracks active run ids and derives the displayed count from current active set size.
- Replaces the displayed count reaction when the active count changes, removes it at zero, and caps counts above the configured emoji list to the final configured emoji.
- Uses `unref` on typing timers where supported so keepalive intervals do not keep Node alive.
- Updates Discord config schema, exported config types, UI hints, generated channel config metadata, and docs.

## Evidence

### Rebase/squash proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `e8c72c3ec83ecd1c68702bf48e5aa44be5887f70`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:

```text
dc9c11be917ebdc711b956250aa80a8e5b47bea6
```

Post-rebase local verification included conflict-marker/diff hygiene plus focused branch-specific gates. Final proof marker for this PR:

```text
POST_REBASE_20260623_DISCORD_SUBAGENT_PROGRESS_GATES_OK
```

Focused validation passed locally on branch `feature/discord-subagent-progress`:

```bash
pnpm test extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  src/plugins/wired-hooks-subagent.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts
```

This verifies:

- source/current message id propagation through delivery context and spawn paths
- tracked ACP/native sub-agent progress hook behavior
- default-off behavior when `subagentProgress.enabled` is missing or false
- enabled Discord count reaction add/replace/remove behavior
- failure marker behavior for non-OK completion
- per-thread tracker separation and typing target behavior
- count capping above the configured ordinal list
- progress cleanup for deferred completion paths
- narrow public hook metadata behavior
- Discord config schema validation

Static/config/docs gates passed locally:

```bash
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm docs:list
git diff --check
```

## Manual Live Gate

Live Discord API proof was run from the installed PR stack on Dev-01 using `OpenClaw 2026.6.10-beta.1` at local HEAD `f15c88bd6f9 stack: combine beta memory slots and Discord progress`, with:

- `channels.discord.subagentProgress.enabled=true`
- `channels.discord.subagentProgress.reactions.enabled=true`
- `channels.discord.subagentProgress.typing.enabled=true`

Observed live behavior in Discord:

- **Normal channel success:** a Discord-originated parent turn spawned a tracked sub-agent run (`8c6dbfa0-e021-4c3a-9992-328de22caa13`). The source Discord message showed the active count reaction while the child was running, typing stayed active during child work, and the count reaction was removed after the child completed. Child proof line: `NORMAL_SUCCESS_1_DONE 2026-06-22T04:48:33Z`.
- **Thread-bound success:** a thread-bound Discord sub-agent run (`01fa6443-4fc1-409d-9cf3-969ce433edbd`) completed successfully while progress was shown from the Discord parent context. Child proof line: `THREAD_SUCCESS_1_DONE 2026-06-22T04:49:50Z`.
- **Concurrent count replacement:** two overlapping tracked sub-agent runs (`4b2701ef-85b0-41d5-a87f-f72924e21869` and `9a5b1e70-5b3b-4820-bb5f-cb6cb7b2a926`) exercised count add/replace/remove behavior. The Discord source message count reaction incremented while both children were active, decremented as the first child completed, and cleared after the second child completed. Child proof lines: `CONCURRENT_SUCCESS_2_DONE 2026-06-22T05:06:23Z` and `CONCURRENT_SUCCESS_3_DONE 2026-06-22T05:06:36Z`.
- **Typing keepalive/stop:** Discord typing was visible while tracked sub-agents were active and stopped after tracked work drained. Earlier long-running proof passes also hit the configured typing safety TTL, with gateway logs showing `[typing] TTL exceeded (60000ms), auto-stopping typing indicator`, confirming the timeout guard executes.

Additional observation: after the final child drains, there can be a brief visual gap before the parent posts its final reconciliation response. That appears to be parent-yield lifecycle behavior rather than this PR's sub-agent progress tracker; it is being tracked separately as a follow-up investigation and is not treated as a blocker for this PR.

Additional durable failure-marker proof was captured from a real Discord source message after a true non-OK sub-agent lifecycle:

- **Failure marker:** source message `1518528276031602788` in Discord channel `1507934992699756645` retained the configured durable failure reaction `🔴` with count `1` and `me: true` after the non-OK sub-agent lifecycle. The source message timestamp was `2026-06-22T08:09:13.897Z`. A later follow-up heartbeat at `2026-06-22T08:29:48.355Z` reported a separate verification pass, confirming the red marker was left on the original source message rather than being ordinary final text delivery.

Redacted observation:

```json
{
  "channel_id": "1507934992699756645",
  "message_id": "1518528276031602788",
  "timestampUtc": "2026-06-22T08:09:13.897Z",
  "content": "Extensively check over the branch end-to-end against the recommended fix and prior investigation/research to ensure everything is clean, finalized, and polished.",
  "reactions": [
    {
      "emoji": { "name": "🔴" },
      "count": 1,
      "me": true
    }
  ]
}
```

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 29 files, +846 / -81
Tests: 11 files, +1232 / -20
Total: 40 files, +2078 / -101

## Rebase / Proof Refresh (2026-06-23)

- Base: `654544b6b7c49d78afbacacd4861bcc830172780`
- Head after refresh: `2be5cc3716a5515ac432c763eff1db472d7e32c9`
- Conflict resolution: Resolved `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` budget conflict by keeping the PR-added SDK surface counts on top of current `main`.
- Validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-cache-pr95604 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts src/utils/delivery-context.test.ts` -> passed, 3 files / 77 tests across 2 shards.
- Push: force-with-lease to `kklouzal/openclaw:feature/discord-subagent-progress` completed.

target.body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

After upgrading from 2026.5.20 to 2026.6.10 google-vertex does not recognize Gemini 3.5 flash mode. When trying to use it, it throws an error.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Setup google enterprise (vertex) ADC
2. Configure the model `google-vertex/gemini-3.5-flash`
3. Try to use it and it will throw an error


### Expected behavior

It should work

### Actual behavior

⚠️ Agent failed before reply: Unknown model: google-vertex/gemini-3.5-flash. Found agents.defaults.models["google-vertex/gemini-3.5-flash"], but no matching models.providers["google-vertex"].models[] entry. Add { "id": "gemini-3.5-flash", "name": "gemini-3.5-flash" } to models.providers["google-vertex"].models[] to register this provider model. For custom or proxy providers, also set api and baseUrl so requests route to the intended endpoint. See https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/model-providers.
Logs: openclaw logs --follow

### OpenClaw version

2026.6.10

### Operating system

Ubuntu 26.04

### Install method

npm global

### Model

gemini 3.5 flash

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> gemini adc

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Affected: everyone using ADC
Severity: unusable
Frequency: always
Consequence: model is unusable

### Additional information
Gemini 2.5 flash works, it doesn't seem to recognize that gemini 3.5 flash is a valid model

_No response_

target.title: fix: keep yielded parent runs deferred until subagents settle
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where parent-agent runs that spawned child sessions/subagents and then called `sessions_yield` could look finished before the parent task actually completed.

The most visible failures were:

- isolated cron `agentTurn` jobs with valid delivery targets could finish as `ok / not-delivered` because the yielded parent had no final payload yet;
- Discord source-message lifecycle could clear parent progress/done indicators while child work was still active or while the parent was waiting to reconcile final output;
- run history/status surfaces made yielded parent work look like delivery failure, ordinary success with no output, or completed source-message work instead of a paused/resumable parent lifecycle.

`session_yield` is supposed to end the current model turn, not the parent task. This PR makes that distinction explicit for the affected runtime consumers.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change promotes yielded-parent semantics into a shared parent lifecycle classifier and routes the affected cron, subagent-registry, and Discord/source-message paths through that state instead of letting each consumer infer completion from terminal-shaped model-attempt events, missing final text, or abort-shaped control flow.

The shipped behavior is:

- yielded parent + active descendants is treated as waiting/deferred, not ordinary completion;
- yielded parent + no descendant is treated as diagnostic error;
- cron delivery waits for a real final outcome instead of counting the yielded attempt as `ok / not-delivered`;
- Discord/source-message progress remains parent-owned while the yielded parent is waiting;
- descendant settlement wakes/reconciles yielded parents through the existing continuation paths.

## User Impact

Operators and developers can now rely on `sessions_yield` after spawning child/subagent work without the parent run being misrepresented as complete at the cron or Discord lifecycle boundary.

Practically:

- autonomous development crons stop inflating misleading `ok / not-delivered` results for intentionally yielded runs;
- cron history exposes a distinct `deferred` state for yielded parent handoff;
- Discord progress no longer marks the parent source message done just because the parent yielded while children continue;
- yielded parent runs are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles;
- debugging points to the parent/child lifecycle state instead of falsely implying delivery target resolution failed.

## Linked Context

- #95535 — `sessions_yield` isolated cron resumption/delivery gap.
- #92369 — subagent orchestration in isolated cron sessions needs reliable spawn/wait/aggregate semantics.
- #92116 — `sessions_yield` completion delivered as passive context can break orchestration loops.
- #86537 — subagent terminal reconciliation can expire suspended final delivery.
- #86538 — session write-lock timeouts can block subagent delivery lanes.
- #95833 — subagent abort-settle/session-lock failure can break session continuation.
- #92460 — isolated cron completion delivery gap around final controller return.
- #95604 — Discord subagent progress visibility.
- #94153 — durable queued subagent completion announcements.
- #95847 — requester-consumed descendant completion accounting.

## Implementation Summary

### Shared parent lifecycle state

- Adds `src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.ts`.
- Classifies lifecycle events with explicit yielded/waiting vs terminal semantics.
- Exports the classifier through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-runtime`.
- Routes subagent registry and Discord source-message handling through the classifier instead of local `yielded && paused` inference.

### Cron lifecycle and delivery

- Adds first-class cron run status `deferred`.
- Classifies yielded descendant handoff before delivery-mode branching, so text, structured payloads, `delivery.mode: none`, source-satisfied delivery, and normal direct delivery all share the same semantics.
- Prevents duplicate/interim parent announcements while descendants are active.
- Records yielded-without-descendant as explicit error diagnostic.
- Keeps deferred manual task runs active rather than failing them.
- Disables one-shot deferred cron jobs after handoff to avoid retry loops.

### Persistence, protocol, CLI, and UI

- Extends run-log JSON/SQLite encoding and status filtering for `deferred`.
- Extends Gateway protocol schemas and cron server filters.
- Extends CLI status/wait typing and docs.
- Extends hook-visible status typing.
- Adds Control UI status/filter labels and generated locale fallbacks.

### Parent/subagent reconciliation

- Tracks yielded/paused parents that still have pending descendants.
- Marks yielded parents with `wakeOnDescendantSettle` so they are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles.
- Handles child success/failure settlement through existing subagent lifecycle recovery paths.
- Adds regression coverage for yielded parents being reconciled after child failure so diagnostics can wake the parent.

### Discord/source-message lifecycle

- Projects lifecycle events from agent execution into reply/channel handling.
- Uses the shared classifier to identify yielded/waiting parent state.
- Preserves in-progress/progress representation for yielded parents with active children.
- Avoids prematurely marking Discord status reactions done or clearing draft/progress state when the parent yielded and remains waiting.

### Concurrent user-message semantics

`docs/tools/subagents.md` documents the selected policy: if a human sends another message while a requester is yielded, the new message starts a normal turn for the same requester session. OpenClaw keeps the yielded run visible as waiting on descendant completion rather than converting it into a stale final reply; when descendant completion arrives, the completion event is handed back to the requester for synthesis.

## Evidence

### Rebase/squash proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `a8aa8bfb134e2b7d5ce77510dc076c13dfd0e4a0`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:

```text
dc9c11be917ebdc711b956250aa80a8e5b47bea6
```

Post-rebase local verification included conflict-marker/diff hygiene plus focused branch-specific gates. Final proof marker for this PR:

```text
POST_REBASE_20260623_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_SDK_SURFACE_REMEDIATED_OK
```

### Focused test coverage

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts \
  src/cron/service/ops.test.ts \
  src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts \
  src/cron/run-log.test.ts \
  packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
```

Passed.

Coverage includes:

- shared parent lifecycle classifier behavior;
- yielded cron parent + active descendants -> `deferred`;
- yielded cron parent + no descendant -> diagnostic `error`;
- structured/no-delivery/source-satisfied delivery paths classified before delivery;
- no duplicate parent interim delivery while descendants are active;
- deferred run-log persistence and filtering;
- deferred Gateway protocol validators;
- manual `cron.run` deferred task-ledger behavior;
- one-shot deferred cron disabling;
- Control UI deferred status/filter rendering;
- CLI wait/status behavior for deferred;
- yielded parent descendant-settle wake/reconciliation;
- yielded parent child-failure diagnostics;
- Discord yielded-parent progress preservation;
- plugin SDK subpath export coverage for the classifier.

### Type, docs, and formatting gates

```bash
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
pnpm tsgo:test:packages --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/tools/subagents.md
git diff --check
```

Passed.

Final local gate marker:

```text
FINAL_PR_READY_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_GATES_OK
```

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 63 files, +470 / -111
Tests: 10 files, +401 / -15
Total: 73 files, +871 / -126


### Live Dev-01 behavior proof — 2026-06-23 UTC

Runtime: Dev-01 installed stack commit `5c1fd341c89b1493fed1ea6014388394291450c3` via `/home/kklouzal/update.sh`; `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (5c1fd34)` and `openclaw-gateway.service` was active from that stack.

Focused live cron/subagent handoff run:

```bash
openclaw cron add --json \
  --name pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z \
  --at +6h --keep-after-run --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --tools sessions_spawn,sessions_yield --timeout-seconds 600 \
  --message 'Live proof marker pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z ...'

openclaw cron run 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 \
  --wait --wait-timeout 12m --poll-interval 5s --timeout 600000
```

Redacted observed output from the live run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938:1782253518340:1",
  "completed": true,
  "status": "deferred",
  "run": {
    "status": "deferred",
    "diagnostics": {
      "summary": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active",
      "entries": [
        {
          "source": "delivery",
          "severity": "warn",

}
cron_run_exit=1
```

The non-zero wait exit is expected for this proof: `openclaw cron run --wait` exits non-zero for persisted `deferred` status, while still returning the run record. Follow-up inspection showed the spawned child completed with marker `CHILD_MARKER pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z` and the shared state row recorded `ended_reason = subagent-complete`. The cron shared-state row for the same job persisted `last_run_status = deferred`, `last_delivery_status = not-requested`, and diagnostic summary `cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active`.

Proof sources:

- CLI run output captured in the maintainer live shell on Dev-01.
- Cron run log: `openclaw cron runs --id 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 --limit 5`.

commands.author: ZengWen-DT

target.title: fix(memory): keep shared QMD manager alive on active-memory recall timeout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96455. After an `active-memory` embedded recall run times out, chat-session `memory_search` returns `"memory search manager is closed"` even though `openclaw memory search --agent main` keeps working against the same QMD backend. The first closed-manager result can also trip the 60s `memory_search` cooldown, so recovery looks sticky until the gateway restarts.

Root cause: active-memory's recall-timeout cleanup (`scheduleMemorySearchCleanupAfterTimeout`) called `closeActiveMemorySearchManager`, which fully retired the **agent-scoped shared QMD manager** that chat `memory_search` and the startup timers/watchers depend on. A request-scoped recall timeout was tearing down long-lived shared state.

## Why This Change Was Made

The cleanup's legitimate job (introduced in #84048) is to release the **disposable local builtin index/embedding providers** a hung recall can leave open — not to retire the shared manager. The fix keeps the shipped close contract intact and does not grow the SDK surface:

- `closeActiveMemorySearchManager` gains a backward-compatible optional `scope?: "manager" | "index-managers"`. Default `"manager"` is the unchanged full-close behavior. `"index-managers"` releases **only** the disposable local index managers (a narrow `releaseMemoryIndexManagers` path in memory-core) and leaves the shared QMD manager alive.
- active-memory's recall-timeout cleanup now calls `closeActiveMemorySearchManager({ ..., scope: "index-managers" })`.
- No new public SDK export is added (the public callable export count is unchanged), so the prior review's `merge-risk: compatibility` (expanded plugin-facing API surface) no longer applies.

The recall's own in-flight QMD query is already cancelled via the embedded run abort signal, so the preserved manager stays healthy — it is the same long-lived manager CLI search and startup timers use successfully.

Addresses the prior review:
- **Compatibility / API surface:** reworked from a new SDK facade to a backward-compatible optional param — no export-count growth.
- **P1 (active-memory timeout test):** the timeout-cleanup test now asserts the `scope: "index-managers"` call, proving the new path.
- **P2 (absent release hook):** intentional no-op with a documenting comment — falling back to full-close would reintroduce #96455.

## User Impact

Users with `active-memory` enabled and a QMD-backed `memory_search` no longer lose chat memory recall after an active-memory recall timeout. The disposable local index/embedding cleanup from #84048 is preserved; only the over-broad shared-manager teardown is removed. No config or API removal; one additive optional param on an existing facade.

## Evidence

Reworked on latest `upstream/main`. Single commit; +134 / -11 across 13 files; SDK callable-export surface unchanged.

### Real QMD-backed runtime proof (qmd 2.5.3)

Installed real `@tobilu/qmd` 2.5.3 (BM25 `searchMode: "search"`). Drove the **real, un-mocked** memory-core `FallbackMemoryManager` (QMD-backed) through the recall-timeout cleanup path vs the full close:

```
[proof] manager#1: FallbackMemoryManager error: (none)
[proof] BEFORE cleanup: search outcome: {"ok":true,"hits":0}
[proof] AFTER releaseMemoryIndexManagers: sameManager: true
[proof] AFTER cleanup: search outcome: {"ok":true,"hits":0}
[proof] AFTER full close: old-manager search outcome: {"ok":false,"message":"memory search manager is closed"}
[proof] after close, fresh get is new manager: true
```

- After the recall-timeout release path: the **same** shared manager is preserved and `search` stays usable — no `"memory search manager is closed"`.
- After a full close (the old behavior): the manager throws exactly `"memory search manager is closed"` — the issue's literal symptom — and a fresh manager is created.

Real qmd BM25 retrieval is healthy on the manager's own index (the `hits:0` above is a separate qmd multi-collection/BM25-scoring nuance on the manager's filtered CLI path, unrelated to manager liveness):

```
$ qmd search "Falcon launch"   # against the manager's own index.sqlite
qmd://memory-root-main/MEMORY.md:3 #e5bc2f
Title: Memory

Project Falcon launch window opens in March. The methalox engine passed static fire.
```

### Regression tests

```
extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts ... 37 passed
src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts ........................ 12 passed (incl. scope-routing)
src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.test.ts ................. 4 passed  (incl. scope passthrough)
extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts ................... 152 passed (timeout asserts scope)
test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts ............. 7 passed  (callable-export budget unchanged)
```

- `search-manager.test.ts`: full-close still closes the QMD manager + new "releaseMemoryIndexManagers ... keeps the shared qmd manager alive (#96455)".
- `memory-runtime.test.ts`: routes `scope: "index-managers"` to the release path without retiring the shared manager.

### Local validation

```
tsgo:core                          OK
oxlint (changed files)             clean
oxfmt --check (changed files)      clean
plugin-sdk:api:gen + :check        OK (no new callable export)
```

target.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Windows QMD shim path and narrows the image URL work into a guarded provider-download path.

- Resolve Windows `.cmd` shims that point at quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints.
- Use the real QMD package name, `@tobilu/qmd`, for package-bin fallback resolution.
- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.
- Route provider-returned image URLs through guarded HTTP with bounded reads, MIME validation, scoped same-origin transport reuse, configured media limits, and operation-deadline timeouts.
- Dropped the unrelated `node-llama-cpp` dependency/lockfile churn from the original PR scope.

## Behavior addressed

Windows QMD availability checks no longer fail when `qmd.cmd` wraps a quoted absolute Node entrypoint instead of a `%~dp0`-relative entrypoint. OpenAI-compatible URL-backed image generation responses now produce image assets without using raw fetch or broad private-network carryover.

## Real environment tested

- AWS Crabbox native Windows: `cbx_36917d25d801`, run `run_0dd48bf72083`, hydrate workflow `26722175678`.
- AWS Crabbox Windows WSL2: `cbx_92cdb109d982`, run `run_740f8e92e641`, hydrate workflow `26721781232`.
- AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate after rebase: `cbx_0b074a7498e9`, run `run_72f46782c53c`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/docs-list.js
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Native Windows and WSL2 ran the same focused Vitest set on hydrated Crabbox workspaces. The final Linux changed gate was run after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Evidence after fix

- Local post-rebase focused tests: 6 shards passed, 218 tests total.
- Native Windows Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 213 tests total on `run_0dd48bf72083`.
- WSL2 Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 215 tests total on `run_740f8e92e641`.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed after regenerating the baseline across the final rebase.
- SDK subpath export check passed.
- Autoreview passed clean after the URL-download security/timeout/boundary fixes.

## Observed result after fix

- Quoted absolute Windows `.cmd` QMD shims resolve to direct Node execution without shell fallback.
- Scoped QMD package fallback resolves through `@tobilu/qmd`.
- URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image responses are accepted and converted into generated image assets.
- Cross-origin provider-returned URLs do not inherit provider private-network/dispatcher/auth policy.
- Same-origin provider-returned URLs keep provider auth/transport policy and still use guarded fetch.
- URL downloads honor configured generated-media byte limits and share the image operation deadline instead of resetting timeout per download.

## What was not tested

Full live QMD memory indexing/search was not run. The final `pnpm check:changed` gate is blocked by a current `origin/main` type error in untouched `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts` from `b0679d1f13d`; this PR does not touch channel ingress queue files.

target.title: Fix config set slash args
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the shared command definition when Discord native command handlers rebuild `/config` text so slash args keep their parser and formatter.
- Preserve Telegram native command payloads from raw message text when the framework-provided payload loses bracketed spans.
- Treat non-JSON bracket/object-like config values as literal strings and render config update replies with JSON string labels so stored values are visible.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Prior local proof before the rebase/squash: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/mentions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-args.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`
- Prior local proof before the rebase/squash: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `/config set` should preserve literal string values, including quoted and unquoted bracketed values, instead of dropping them or reporting a missing/invalid value.

Real environment tested: Telegram Desktop against the local gateway running this worktree as `OPENCLAW_REPO_DIR`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent `/config set messages.responsePrefix="[cu-fixed2]"`, `/config show messages.responsePrefix`, `/config set messages.responsePrefix=[cu-unquoted-fixed]`, and `/config unset messages.responsePrefix` in Telegram Desktop.

Evidence after fix: Telegram replies reported `Config updated: messages.responsePrefix="[cu-fixed2]"`, `Config messages.responsePrefix: "[cu-fixed2]"`, and `Config updated: messages.responsePrefix="[cu-unquoted-fixed]"`.

Observed result after fix: Both quoted and unquoted bracketed prefix values persisted as literal strings, and cleanup removed the key.

What was not tested: The test suite was not rerun after the final rebase/squash per instruction; only `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` was run after the final commit.

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve thread reply target through delivery
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** Google Chat replies could leave the inbound Chat thread and appear as new top-level messages. - **Why it matters:** delayed or tool-driven assistant replies break group-thread UX when they detach from the conversation the user spoke into. - **What changed:** preserve `message.thread.name` as `MessageThreadId`, expose it to reply/message-tool threading, and normalize Google Chat outbound thread targets before they reach `thread.name`. - **What did NOT change:** no Google Chat per-thread OpenClaw session isolation; core reply plumbing remains channel-agnostic and Google Chat resource-name handling stays behind the Google Chat boundary. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #69422 - Related #39554 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Google Chat assistant replies from existing Chat threads should remain in the inbound thread. - Real environment tested: Linux VPS-hosted OpenClaw Google Chat runtime with service-account auth and a real Google Chat space. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Jai sent real Google Chat threaded messages and confirmed assistant replies stayed in-thread before this PR was marked ready. - Evidence after fix: screenshot evidence is attached below: - Before: Google Chat reply appeared outside the inbound thread. - After: Google Chat reply stayed inside the inbound thread. - Scope note: typing-placeholder editing is separate follow-up behavior; this PR fixes thread placement. - Observed result after fix: live Google Chat replies stayed in-thread; local seam tests also verified natural replies, message-tool sends, and current-reply behavior use the inbound Google Chat thread resource. - What was not tested: every Google Chat account/space policy combination; typing-placeholder edit/cleanup for message-tool sends; Google Chat per-thread OpenClaw session isolation, which is out of scope. - Before evidence: prior behavior re-rooted replies as new top-level Chat messages. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: Google Chat exposes different resource shapes for messages (`spaces/.../messages/...`) and threads (`spaces/.../threads/...`), and OpenClaw previously let message-shaped values flow into thread-routing seams. - Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered legacy single-resource channels, but not a provider where current message id and current thread id are different resource names. - Contributing context: - Inbound webhook context did not populate generic `MessageThreadId` from `message.thread.name`. - The message-tool path had no inbound-thread default when the agent omitted `threadId`. - `[[reply_to_current]]` resolves to the current message id; for Google Chat that value must be converted to the inbound thread before delivery. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-plumbing.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.webhook-routing.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: Google Chat has a current message resource and a distinct current thread resource; implicit replies and message-tool sends use the thread resource, while message-shaped reply ids are rewritten or rejected before reaching the Chat API. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers webhook ingress, core reply seams, message-tool routing, channel delivery, and the API boundary without requiring live Google Chat in CI. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Google Chat assistant replies from an existing Chat thread now stay in that thread instead of creating a new top-level message. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: Google Chat webhook -> currentMessageId=spaces/.../messages/... -> delivery sends that as thread.name -> Chat API rejects/falls back -> new top-level thread After: Google Chat webhook -> MessageThreadId=spaces/.../threads/... -> reply/tool delivery resolves outbound thread -> Chat API receives thread.name=spaces/.../threads/... ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS local checkout; Linux VPS-hosted runtime for real Chat verification - Runtime/container: OpenClaw source checkout; local verification on exact rebased head `a617644c81dd43bb7285d3001ca6d19750e62e68`; live Google Chat verification on a Linux VPS-hosted runtime with the thread-placement patch applied - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Google Chat service-account auth - Relevant config (redacted): Google Chat enabled with group mention policy; no secret/config changes required ### Steps 1. Send a threaded Google Chat message that mentions the bot. 2. Let the assistant produce a normal final reply. 3. Exercise message-tool sends without explicit `threadId`. 4. Exercise `[[reply_to_current]]` behavior where the core seam supplies the current message id. ### Expected - Replies and message-tool sends stay in the original Google Chat thread. - Explicit thread-shaped ids are preserved. - Message-shaped or malformed thread targets never reach `thread.name` as-is. ### Actual - Before this patch: replies could re-root as new top-level Chat messages. - After this patch: live verification and local seam coverage keep replies in the inbound thread. - Scope note: typing-placeholder editing is separate follow-up behavior and is not fixed by this PR. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Screenshot evidence attached below: - Before screenshot: Google Chat reply escaped the inbound thread and appeared outside the original conversation thread. - After screenshot: Google Chat reply stayed inside the inbound thread. - Scope note: the after screenshot may still show a stale typing placeholder when the message-tool path is used; typing-placeholder edit/cleanup is not part of this PR. Verification commands run on exact rebased head `a617644c81dd43bb7285d3001ca6d19750e62e68` after the May 26 rebase: ```bash mise exec node@22.22.2 -- node scripts/docs-list.js # passed mise exec node@22.22.2 -- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts extensions/googlechat/src/channel.adapters.ts extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/google-auth.runtime.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/google-auth.runtime.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-reply-delivery.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.webhook-routing.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/thread-resource.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-payloads-base.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-plumbing.test.ts # All matched files use the correct format. mise exec node@22.22.2 -- node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/google-auth.runtime.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.webhook-routing.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-plumbing.test.ts # 2 Vitest shards passed; 330 tests passed mise exec node@22.22.2 -- node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json # lanes=core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, docs ``` Additional broad test signal from pre-ready validation on a prior PR head: ```bash pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply extensions/googlechat extensions/telegram # auto-reply: 113 files, 1508 tests passed # telegram: 106 files, 1483 tests passed # googlechat shard was rerun after fixture alignment: 15 files, 141 tests passed ``` ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Jai verified live Google Chat thread placement on a real Google Chat runtime and attached before/after screenshots below. - I verified the exact rebased code locally with the commands listed above. - Edge cases checked: - `[[reply_to_current]]` can still resolve to the core current message id; Google Chat rewrites message-shaped values at the channel boundary. - Explicit thread-shaped `replyToId` wins over the inbound thread. - Malformed thread values are rejected before reaching the Google Chat send API. - Message-tool sends without `threadId` use the inbound thread only when the current target matches and reply mode allows implicit threading. - What you did **not** verify: - Every Google Chat account/space policy combination. - Per-thread OpenClaw session isolation for Google Chat; this PR does not implement it. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: callers that accidentally pass a Google Chat message resource as `thread` now fail loudly instead of silently falling back. - Mitigation: channel delivery rewrites known message-resource reply targets to the inbound thread; the API guard reports invalid values before sending. - Risk: a future non-message, non-thread Google Chat resource shape could appear in `replyToId`. - Mitigation: only `spaces/.../threads/...` passes as `thread.name`; everything else is dropped or falls back to the inbound thread. --- ## Screenshots (Bad, Good) ### Bad (before patch) ### Good (after patch)

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve group route for mirrored oc replies
target.body:
Refs #58537. This PR fixes the normal mirrored Feishu group-reply route path, but intentionally does not auto-close the broader issue because proactive bare-target sends and duplicate-session cleanup may remain separate maintainer scope.

## Summary

- preserve Feishu group session identity when a delivery-mirror reply resolves a bare `oc_...` target from the matching current Feishu group session
- trust directory-resolved Feishu group targets when building outbound session routes
- keep ambiguous bare `oc_...` targets direct when there is no group context, preserving the existing compatibility boundary
- keep bare Feishu user/open IDs direct when shared target resolution only supplied a normalized fallback group hint

## Why this shape

`oc_...` IDs are compatibility-sensitive because prior history says they can be ambiguous. This fix does not revive the broad "all bare oc_ is group" rule. It only promotes the route to group when OpenClaw already has group evidence: either the current session key is the same Feishu group conversation, or target resolution came from a directory-backed group match. Normalized fallback hints are not trusted for group promotion, so bare Feishu user/open IDs remain direct.

## Public surface

- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.
- No migration or startup reconciliation behavior added.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
A normal Feishu group reply/delivery-mirror path can recreate a phantom `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_...` session because the route resolver sees a bare `oc_...` target and loses the existing group identity.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, OpenClaw checkout `/root/src/openclaw-87774-proof`, PR head `245e28e44bc3a8d7bc774690dbd2bf84212b6b12`, production Feishu channel route resolver `extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts:resolveFeishuOutboundSessionRoute`, and focused Feishu/outbound runtime tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Ran a source harness through the production Feishu resolver for three redacted route/session cases: matching current Feishu group session, directory-resolved group target, and normalized fallback user target.
2. Reran focused Feishu/outbound validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/delivery-info.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
3. Reran changed-file static checks: `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`, `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "245e28e44bc3a8d7bc774690dbd2bf84212b6b12",
  "productionResolver": "extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts:resolveFeishuOutboundSessionRoute",
  "proof": [
    {
      "label": "matching-current-feishu-group-session",
      "input": {
        "target": "oc_group_chat",
        "currentSessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat:topic:om_root:sender:ou_user"
      },
      "route": {
        "sessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
        "baseSessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
        "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "oc_group_chat" },
        "chatType": "group",
        "from": "feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
        "to": "oc_group_chat"
      },
      "noDirectPhantomForOcGroup": true
    },
    {
      "label": "directory-resolved-group-target",
      "input": {
        "target": "oc_directory_chat",
        "resolvedTarget": { "kind": "group", "to": "oc_directory_chat", "source": "directory" }
      },
      "route": {
        "sessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_directory_chat",
        "baseSessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_directory_chat",
        "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "oc_directory_chat" },
        "chatType": "group",
        "from": "feishu:group:oc_directory_chat",
        "to": "oc_directory_chat"
      },
      "noDirectPhantomForOcGroup": true
    },
    {
      "label": "normalized-fallback-user-stays-direct",
      "input": {
        "target": "ou_user",
        "resolvedTarget": { "kind": "group", "to": "ou_user", "source": "normalized" }
      },
      "route": {
        "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
        "baseSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
        "peer": { "kind": "direct", "id": "ou_user" },
        "chatType": "direct",
        "from": "feishu:ou_user",
        "to": "ou_user"
      },
      "noDirectPhantomForOcGroup": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The production Feishu resolver preserves `feishu:group:oc_...` session identity when the current session proves the bare `oc_...` target is the active group, and when a directory-backed target resolution proves a Feishu group. It does not promote normalized fallback user/open IDs to group routes.

Supporting checks:
- Focused Feishu/outbound Vitest passed: 5 files, 218 tests.
- Targeted `oxfmt --check` passed.
- Targeted `oxlint` passed with 0 warnings/errors.
- `git diff --check` passed.

What was not tested:
No live Feishu/Lark OpenChat account was available in this session, so this proof does not include an actual Feishu network send. Proactive context-free sends and duplicate-session cleanup remain out of scope for this PR.

target.title: Add Telegram per-group UI overrides
target.body:
## Summary
- add Telegram per-group/topic overrides for UI streaming settings (`streaming.mode`, preview/progress toolProgress) and `ackReaction`
- resolve Telegram scoped overrides before account/channel defaults
- honor explicit `delivery.mode="none"` on main-session cron system events without waking the assistant

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/group-config-helpers.test.ts src/agents/identity.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram chats can carry per-group/topic overrides for `streaming.mode` and `ackReaction` that resolve ahead of account/channel defaults, so a chat configured with `streaming.mode="off"` and `ackReaction=null` no longer leaks intermediate tool-progress chrome (`Image:`, `Exec:`, progress markers) or applies acknowledgment reactions. Independently, main-session cron jobs configured with `delivery.mode="none"` no longer wake the operator's direct chat or generate visible system-event traffic.

Real environment tested: production OpenClaw gateway on a Hetzner-class VPS (Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS, runtime `openclaw-gateway v2026.5.6` patched with the changes from this PR), with a real Telegram supergroup configured under the new per-group block. Vendista cron jobs (`vendista-events-poll` every 30 min, `vendista-terminals-health` every 6 h) reconfigured to `payload.kind: agentTurn`, `sessionTarget: isolated`, `delivery: { mode: "none" }`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: deployed the patched runtime; `openclaw-gateway.service` restarted at `2026-05-28T20:02:48 CEST` (verified via `systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway`, line `Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-05-28 20:02:48 CEST`). Applied per-group config to `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with the new schema fields (`streaming.mode`, `ackReaction`); runtime parsed the new fields after deploy, whereas the same JSON shape was rejected by the pre-patch runtime ten times in a row at `2026-05-28T11:20–11:21 CEST` with `invalid config: must NOT have additional properties`. Sent a test photo into the group from a real Telegram client at `2026-05-28T20:10 CEST`. Pulled `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "yesterday"` (1802 lines) for the post-deploy window, grepped for the chat id (66 matches) and for chrome leak markers (zero matches). Live runtime config dumped via `jq '.channels.telegram.groups."<group-chat-id>"' ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

Evidence after fix: the new fields `streaming` and `ackReaction` (introduced by this PR) are present and accepted by the patched runtime — `jq` output for the group block shows `"streaming": { "mode": "off" }` and `"ackReaction": null` alongside the preserved `requireMention: false`, `groupPolicy: "open"`, and `systemPrompt` (silent-observer policy, content redacted from this PR body). Pre-patch runtime rejected the identical config ten times (`May 28 11:20:36 GAS node[1378495]: channels.telegram.groups.<id>: invalid config: must NOT have additional properties` — and nine more sequential PID rejections through `11:21:09`); post-patch runtime accepted the same JSON with no rejection lines. The test photo turn entered the patched runtime as a fresh embedded run for the group: `May 28 20:10:09 [plugins] openclaw-mem0: registered (mode: open-source, user: openclaw-main, autoRecall: true, autoCapture: true, skills: true)` → `May 28 20:10:19 [agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] prep stages: runId=5e14b0bf-dfe7-4473-bc90-27485f002454 sessionId=c70bc90a-9b36-46da-aa2e-ff750f2a7b57 phase=stream-ready totalMs=9835` → `May 28 20:10:22 [diagnostic] liveness ... work=[active=agent:main:telegram:group:<group-chat-id>(processing/embedded_run,q=1,age=3s last=embedded_run:started)]` → `May 28 20:10:24 [plugins] openclaw-mem0: skills-mode recall (strategy=smart) injecting 1 memories (~95 tokens)`. PID `29275` is the patched gateway process (started `20:02:48 CEST`). Chrome leak check across the full post-deploy log window (~1h 30min of live operation): `awk '/2026-05-28T20:02:48/,EOF' tmp_log.txt | grep -cE "Nautiling|Image:|Exec:"` returned `0` — no streaming-progress chrome or tool-execution markers reached the log path between deploy and the latest entry. Focused unit suites (supplemental only): `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts`, `config-schema.test.ts`, `group-config-helpers.test.ts`, `src/agents/identity.test.ts` — 57/57 passed; `src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts` — 13/13 passed.

Observed result after fix: in the live Telegram supergroup, the bot processed the test photo through an embedded run on the patched runtime (`runId=5e14b0bf-...`), recalled and stored memory entries through the `openclaw-mem0` plugin, and emitted no streaming-progress chrome strings into the gateway log path. The embedded run for the group completed without any subsequent `tool:image:started` or streaming-progress events in the same turn, consistent with the configured `streaming.mode="off"`. The previously failing schema-validation errors against the new per-group fields stopped after the patched runtime was deployed, demonstrating that the new fields are accepted at the config schema level and resolved at the per-group scope ahead of account/channel defaults.

What was not tested: cross-account interaction where multiple Telegram accounts override the same chat simultaneously — the current change resolves at the group/topic scope ahead of account-level defaults, but multi-account contention is out of scope for this patch. Specific delivery of Vendista cron isolated turns into a sink-only session was not separately captured in the post-deploy window pulled here; the cron portion is supported by the focused unit suite above.

target.title: fix(control-ui): improve session key display for direct chats with long identifiers
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Long session identifiers (>20 chars) in direct chat session keys overflow the sessions table and detail panel, making the Control UI hard to read
- The hover tooltip shows the same truncated value, losing the full session key context

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #61213: Users with Feishu/long-ID channels see keys like `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` in every cell, wasting horizontal space

What is the intended outcome?

- Long identifiers are truncated to `first_6...last_4` format in visible cells
- The full raw session key is preserved in the hover/tooltip for copy/debug access (fallback rows only)
- Sessions with identity labels keep hover = visible label (preserves existing behavior)
- Sessions with displayNames are unchanged (displayName stays as secondary, never promoted to main cell)
- The canonical `parseSessionKey` function remains unchanged; truncation only happens via the new `shortenSessionKeyForCell` helper

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to the chat page session picker, sidebar, or overview cards
- No changes to the `parseSessionKey` / `resolveSessionDisplayName` APIs
- No runtime config or user preference for truncation length

What does success look like?

- `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` renders as `Feishu · ou_670...27b2` in the cell
- Hovering on fallback rows (no identity) shows the full raw key
- Hovering on identity rows shows the visible label (friendlyKeyLabel)
- `agent:main:telegram:direct:user_123` (short) renders as `Telegram · user_123` unchanged
- All 47 related unit tests pass

What should reviewers focus on?

- `ui/src/ui/session-display.ts`: new `shortenSessionKeyForCell` function (lines 31-50)
- `ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts:840-846`: `hoverTitle` and `keyCellTitle` logic
- `ui/src/ui/session-display.test.ts`: 21 tests covering both canonical and cell-specific behavior

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Related to #61213 (partial fix - truncation implemented, binding.comment and agent.name precedence out of scope)

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- PR #94813 (this PR)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No; self-identified contributor fix

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Long session identifiers overflow the sessions table cells in the Control UI. The raw key is now only visible on hover for fallback rows, not in the cell text.

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout, `ui` directory vitest project.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Unit tests for the new helper
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts

# Existing sessions UI regression tests
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
✓ src/ui/session-display.test.ts (21 tests)
✓ src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts (26 tests)
```

- `parseSessionKey("agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2")` returns full identifier (canonical)
- `shortenSessionKeyForCell("agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2")` returns `"Feishu · ou_670...27b2"` (truncated)
- Hover on fallback rows: raw key; Hover on identity rows: visible label

**Screenshots:** See previous comments.

**Observed result after fix:** Long session identifiers like `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` now display as `Feishu · ou_670...27b2` in the sessions table cells. Hovering on fallback rows (no identity) shows the full raw key in the tooltip. Hovering on identity rows shows the friendly label. Short identifiers (<=20 chars) remain unchanged.

**What was not tested:** Live runtime proof with actual Feishu/Telegram/long-ID channel sessions in a running OpenClaw instance. This change was validated via unit tests only (47 tests passing). The UI truncation logic is visually verified through existing test assertions on the rendered output.

## Tests

```bash
# New + existing unit tests
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts
# → 47 passed
```

## Risk

| Question | Yes / No |
|---|---|
| Does this change affect an existing public API (config, CLI, plugin SDK, gateway protocol)? | No |
| Does this change affect an upgrade path or require a migration? | No |
| Could this change break existing user workflows or persisted state? | No — pure UI display change, no state or config changes |
| Does this change introduce a new dependency or modify an existing one? | No |

**Risk description:** Low risk. Pure frontend display change with dedicated regression tests.

**Mitigations:** 
- Canonical `parseSessionKey` unchanged (5 existing consumers unaffected)
- `shortenSessionKeyForCell` is new and scoped to Sessions table only
- displayName stays secondary, never promoted to main cell
- Identity rows preserve existing hover behavior

## Current review state

- [x] Code changes complete
- [x] Unit tests: 21 new + 26 existing = 47 passing
- [x] Format check: all files correct
- [ ] Reviewer: awaiting maintainer review

## Summary

- Add a bundled Anthropic loader guardrail proving `claude-cli` is registered and resolvable as a CLI backend.
- Assert the same test does not register `claude-cli` as an embedded agent harness, matching the current runtime-family split.
- Add a legacy Anthropic CLI migration guardrail for `claude-cli/*` model refs plus old `embeddedHarness.runtime: "claude-cli"` configs, verifying they migrate to canonical `anthropic/*` model refs with `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"`.

## Scope

This PR is now test-only. It keeps the current design where CLI backends and embedded agent harnesses are separate runtime families. It no longer promotes CLI backends into the agent harness registry.

Fixes #72576

## Validation
